text_blocks.text: The university operates 12
research institutes and 113 research centers
on campus. Among these are the Oriental
Institute--a museum and research center
for Near Eastern studies owned and
operated by the university--and a number
of National Resource Centers, including the
Center for Middle Eastern Studies. Chicago
also operates or is affiliated with a
number of research institutions apart from
the university proper. The university
partially manages Argonne National
Laboratory, part of the United States
Department of Energy's national laboratory
system, and has a joint stake in
Fermilab, a nearby particle physics
laboratory, as well as a stake in the
Apache Point Observatory in Sunspot, New
Mexico. Faculty and students at the
adjacent Toyota Technological Institute at
Chicago collaborate with the university, In
2013, the university announced that it was
affiliating the formerly independent Marine
Biological Laboratory in Woods Hole,
Mass. Although formally unrelated, the
National Opinion Research Center is located
on Chicago's campus.
